llm_details.1a_reason: Music features are identified per section and some beat-level matches are stated (e.g., drums enter on 3, guitar on 5; chorus crash on 25; cowbell 2&4; snare references). However, many correspondences remain generalized (“matches hi-hat pulse”, “hits the snare pocket”) without consistently mapping exact instrument accents to exact counts across the whole phrase set, and later sections (Bridge/Chorus2/Outro) have no count-by-count movement shown to prove correspondence.
llm_details.1b_reason: There are multiple explicit executable off-beat actions: Scooby Doo weight transfer on “& of 5” and “& of 13”, which-a-way pivot on “& of 17”, point on “& of 27”, hand snap/freeze on “& of 37”, plus strum on “&” in 29-&-30. Syncopation is present and specified, but finer subdivisions (e/a) are not used and some off-beat claims are loosely justified by assumed ghost notes rather than clearly described musical hits.
llm_details.1c_reason: The mapping describes an intended arc across Intro→Verse→Chorus→Bridge→Chorus2→Outro, but only Intro, Verse 1, and Chorus 1 have detailed counts. Without Bridge/Chorus2/Outro choreography, differentiation and completion of the full-structure emotional arc cannot be verified; energy progression is asserted more than demonstrated.
llm_details.2a_reason: Vocabulary is predominantly Locking (points, wrist rolls, Scooby Doo, stop & go, which-a-way, pacing, skeeter rabbit, Leo walk, locks). Some descriptions drift toward non-standard/ambiguous technique labeling for Locking (e.g., “chest lock” framed with strong contraction reads closer to popping/animation language; “knee lock” description is vague/atypical), but overall it stays mostly within Locking character and presentation.
llm_details.2b_reason: Many counts provide clear directions, levels, and facings, making them teachable. Still, several items are under-specified or inconsistent for direct execution (e.g., exact footwork for Leo Walk/pacing steps not fully defined; “synth hit marks the section” but no movement tied; some freezes described without precise duration; some anatomical cues are stylized rather than technical). Missing counts for later song sections also reduce executability as a full routine.
llm_details.2c_reason: Some transitions are logical (point→wrist roll, Scooby Doo travel→lock, call/response travel/retreat). However, there are multiple high-impact jumps and immediate freezes (25-26 jump up lock to deep knee lock; 35-36 skeeter rabbit into twist) with limited prep/landing mechanics described, and a few rapid direction changes (which-a-way chains) that may be abrupt at 115 BPM without clearer weight-transfer guidance.
llm_details.3a_reason: Choreography shows some creative phrasing (triangular rapid-fire points, off-beat roll interrupt freeze, call-and-response spatial logic, instrument-mime integration). But much content remains standard Locking sequencing and the plan leans on descriptive narrative more than truly novel structural devices; missing later sections also limits the perceived originality of a complete work.
llm_details.3b_reason: There is evident layering: facial/character notes, forward/back travel, diagonals, level changes, and front/right/upstage facings. Still, spatial design is not fully developed into a stage path across the full routine (only first ~40 counts detailed), and focus/sight-line choices are mentioned sporadically rather than systematically composed.
llm_details.4a_reason: Not format-compliant with the stated requirement of full 8x8 coverage and the referenced output_format.md/“10 required sections”; only 1-40 counts are choreographed in detail. Additionally, the submission includes extra instructional rubric text and does not present Bridge/Chorus2/Outro count breakdowns, so it cannot meet the required structure/completeness.
